Safety Apr 7, 2026

Southwest Airlines Limiting Passengers To 1 Portable Charger Onboard To Reduce Fire Risk

Southwest Airlines will limit passengers to one power bank from April 20, tightening safety rules amid rising lithium battery fire risks onboard.

Southwest's unilateral restriction signals industry consensus on lithium battery hazards may soon drive coordinated FAA regulations affecting passenger convenience airline-wide.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earthset

The Artemis II crew captured this view of Earth setting on April 6, 2026, as they flew around the Moon. As the astronauts flew over the Moon’s far side, the crew photographed and described terrain features including impact craters, ancient lava flows, and surface cracks and ridges formed as the...

Artemis II's lunar orbit mission demonstrates NASA's sustained human spaceflight capability for deep-space exploration, validating the technical systems required for eventual crewed lunar landing missions.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

Picture of the Day: April 7, 2026

A J-3 Cub spreads its wings over the desert.

The J-3 Cub's enduring presence in modern aviation reflects the aircraft's unmatched capability for operating from unprepared surfaces, making it invaluable for backcountry access and remote operations.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing feasible controls, not just identify hazards.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Apr 7, 2026

Charleston Airshow Moves Amid Ongoing Conflict

Joint Base Charleston says 2026 show was changed to an off-base due to operational requirements tied to current global events.

Military airshows are now subject to operational constraints from global conflicts, potentially affecting traditional defense contractor recruitment and public engagement venues.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Delta Air Lines Becomes 3rd US Carrier To Hike Checked Bag Fees As Fuel Prices Soar

Delta Air Lines joins United and JetBlue in hiking checked baggage fees, following a sharp rise in global jet fuel prices.

Synchronized fee increases across multiple carriers signal that fuel surcharges have reached threshold levels where ancillary revenue becomes critical to offset margin compression.

See Simple Flying →

Military Apr 7, 2026

Kraus Hamdani Aerospace Awarded $270 Million AFCENT Contract to Accelerate Deployment of K1000ULE

Kraus Hamdani Aerospace (KHA) has been awarded a $270 Million single-source IDIQ rapid deployment contract...

Single-source IDIQ awards bypass competitive bidding, suggesting KHA's uncrewed system meets urgent operational needs that competitors cannot match in required timeframe.

NASA's public SLS rollout event represents a strategic shift toward leveraging digital creators and social media to build sustained public engagement for deep-space exploration programs.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS represents a paradigm shift from investigating accidents after they occur to preventing them through systematic hazard identification and mitigation.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Why The Boeing 777-300ER Is Preferred By Passengers

The 777-300ER is one of the most common widebody airliners currently in the skies.

The 777-300ER's passenger preference stems from its superior cabin comfort features and spacious windows, creating competitive advantage for airlines operating long-haul routes.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Intellian advances ESA for Panasonic, reveals further aero aspirations

Having made a name for itself in the maritime industry as a leading provider of flat panel antennas, including in support of Eutelsat OneWeb Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-powered connectivity, Intellian recently burst onto the aviation scene when it was selected by Panasonic Avionics to produce a...

Intellian's transition from maritime to aviation antenna systems signals intensifying competition in airborne satellite connectivity as LEO networks reshape in-flight broadband capabilities.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Modern cabin innovations increasingly prioritize passenger wellness through air quality, lighting, and modular layouts rather than merely luxury aesthetics.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon

Media will have an opportunity at 9:45 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, to speak with NASA’s Artemis II crew members as they continue their journey toward Earth during their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will take place with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman,...

Real-time crew communications during lunar return represent a strategic shift toward public engagement and transparency in deep-space exploration missions.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure at RNO requires heightened pilot vigilance due to specific procedural complexity that differs from standard departure operations at this airport.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Alaska Airlines Makes History: Boeing 737 MAX Flights To Europe Launch Next Month

It will become the carrier's new longest narrowbody service ever, but there's a twist...

Alaska Airlines' transatlantic 737 MAX expansion signals industry confidence in the aircraft's reliability recovery while potentially reshaping narrowbody economics for long-haul routes.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Pilots’ Group Warns of Conflict-Zone Strain

New position paper says repeated uneventful flights should not reduce scrutiny.

Pilots risk normalizing hazard exposure in conflict zones, where operational complacency from routine flights increases accident vulnerability despite apparent safety records.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Press Release: FlightPath3D map gets extra smart with Luci Live launch

The inflight map is now an intelligent, interactive information engine, thanks to the launch of Luci Live. Developed by FlightPath3D, the premier provider of immersive airline passenger maps, and unveiled at Aircraft Interiors Expo in Hamburg this month, Luci Live adds a customizable, AI travel...

AI-powered inflight maps are evolving from static visualization tools into dynamic passenger information platforms, enabling airlines to deliver personalized real-time content and differentiate cabin experiences.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Press Release: WTCE reveals trends reshaping APAC onboard hospitality

World Travel Catering & Onboard Services Expo (WTCE) has released its newest report, exploring the cultural, culinary and commercial forces transforming inflight hospitality across Asia’s rapidly expanding aviation landscape. With Asia-Pacific airlines transporting 390.5 million international...

Asia-Pacific's rapidly expanding aviation market is forcing carriers to balance culturally-specific culinary offerings with operational efficiency as passenger volumes exceed 390 million.

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design inconsistencies at major hubs like LAX create preventable incursion risks that require standardized signage and taxiway geometry improvements across the National Airspace System.

FAA Budget Request Seeks 2,300 Controller Trainees

Agency ties proposed hiring increase to staffing, training and modernization needs.

FAA's trainee expansion signals recognition that current attrition rates and retirement waves require aggressive workforce planning to maintain safe operations amid increasing traffic demands.

Portugal's aviation authorities have shared further details on the incident that occurred two months ago.

Revealing incident details months later suggests systemic safety concerns that may affect broader fleet operations and passenger confidence in European carriers.

Red Cat Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: RCAT) (“Red Cat” or the “Company”), a U.S.-based provider of advanced...

Red Cat's partnership with Arastelle integrates persistent ISR with tactical communications, creating a competitive advantage in the critical defense drone market where real-time data fusion is increasingly essential.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Major PX4 drone software vulnerability raises hijacking concerns

A widely used piece of drone software just got a serious cybersecurity wake-up call, and if you operate drones in the US, it’s something you’ll want to pay attention to. more…...

PX4's vulnerability exposes a critical supply-chain security gap affecting thousands of autonomous systems beyond consumer drones, requiring immediate operator audits and vendor coordination.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation domains could unlock capacity gains comparable to infrastructure investment while simultaneously reducing environmental impact.

See MDPI →

Military Apr 7, 2026

US Army launches Anduril Altius 700 drone from Apache for the first time

The US Army has for the first time launched a drone from an AH-64E Apache attack helicopter, firing…...

Integrating expendable loitering munitions with existing Apache platforms expands standoff strike capability without requiring new helicopter platforms or extensive modifications.

Airport Operator Sued Over Alleged Unpaid Revenue

City alleges missing fees, absent records and diverted tuition receipts at municipal GA airport.

Municipal airports face heightened scrutiny on financial accountability, with allegations of misappropriated revenue potentially triggering regulatory audits across smaller facilities nationwide.

New Details Emerge About Rescue Mission Deep Inside Iran

New details were released during a White House press conference which described the rescue of the crew of DUDE 44, the F-15E Strike Eagle that went down in Iran. The rescue of the crew of the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet shot down over Iran has rapidly emerged as one of the most complex and […]...

Complex multi-asset coordination across hostile territory demonstrates critical operational planning for pilot recovery in denied environments.

Canadian pilot salaries are becoming increasingly competitive as regional operators face recruitment pressures in a recovering post-pandemic aviation market.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Starfish Space raises more than $100 million

Starfish Space has raised more than $100 million to scale up production of its satellite servicing spacecraft.

Increased funding signals growing investor confidence in on-orbit servicing as a sustainable business model for extending satellite lifecycle and reducing space debris.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

Newly anointed NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman made a $20 billion announcement on March 24, 2026. To the delight of space enthusiasts everywhere, Isaacman said NASA was cancelling its project to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and would reorient to building a $20 billion base on the surface...

NASA's pivot to a lunar surface base prioritizes operational resilience over orbital infrastructure, demanding mission-critical cybersecurity protocols that aerospace contractors must integrate from inception.

See SpaceNews →

First refurbished Jetstar 787 Dreamliner takes flight from Melbourne

Jetstar’s first refurbished Boeing 787 Dreamliner launched its inaugural service from Melbourne Airport (MEL) to Phuket International Airport…...

Jetstar's 787 deployment signals budget carriers' shift toward wide-body international routes, expanding low-cost long-haul capacity in Asia-Pacific markets.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

British Airways Flew 13 Hours After Passenger Died Onboard - Foul Smell Complaints Followed

You might expect that, when a passenger passes away during a flight, pilots would divert the aircraft to an airport to deal with the situation. However, this isn’t always the case. Indeed, if a passenger dies of natural causes, airlines may just opt to have the flight continue onward to its...

Airlines can legally continue flights when passengers die of natural causes, creating operational and customer service tensions that require clear protocols.

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Iberia's 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Influenced by external factors, one very long link takes the crown, but what about the rest?

Iberia's ultra-long-range expansion signals profitability potential for 16-hour routes, challenging conventional wisdom about passenger fatigue and airline economics on extreme-distance markets.
